explanation:
The natural language requirement is: 'PCV Mode: The transition from PCV to PSV shall occur if ventilation is on, the transition from PCV to PSV shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time if the change mode command has been received from the GUI.' Atomic propositions are: PCVMode, PSVMode, ventilating, inspiratoryPhaseEnd, and PSVModeSelected. 

We are describing when a transition from PCV mode to PSV mode can happen. Specifically: 
1. If ventilation is on and PCV mode is active (i.e., the system is in 'PCVMode' and 'ventilating'), then the transition to PSV (i.e., 'PSVMode') can occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time ('inspiratoryPhaseEnd') if the mode change command has been received from the GUI ('PSVModeSelected').

Thus, whenever PCVMode is active, ventilating is true, and PSVModeSelected is true, at the moment inspiratoryPhaseEnd becomes true, a transition to PSVMode should follow immediately (i.e., next state). In LTL, this means: 
Globally, whenever all these are true at a step, in the next state PSVMode should become true. 
LTL: G(((PCVMode & ventilating & PSVModeSelected & inspiratoryPhaseEnd) -> X(PSVMode)))

Step-by-step the mapping is: 
- 'if ventilation is on' → ventilating
- 'PCVMode' is active → PCVMode
- 'the transition ... shall occur at the end of PCV inspiratory time' → at inspiratoryPhaseEnd
- 'if the change mode command has been received from GUI' → PSVModeSelected
- 'transition from PCV to PSV shall occur...' → next state is PSVMode
- 'Globally' → G operator
- 'immediately after inspiratoryPhaseEnd event/instant' → X(PSVMode)

This assumes the atomic proposition PSVMode is set in the next state to indicate the transition.

explanation_dict.natural_language: if ventilation is on
PCV Mode active
the change mode command from GUI has been received
end of a PCV inspiratory time
at the next step, the mode is PSVMode
Globally, if all of the above are true at the same time, then at the next step, PSVMode becomes true

explanation:
The English requirement refers to a mode transition: from PCV to PSV. It specifies two conditions: (1) ventilation is on, and (2) at the end of PCV inspiratory time, provided a change mode command has been received from the GUI (which we take as PSVModeSelected). 

We encode this as follows:

- 'The transition from PCV to PSV shall occur if ventilation is on' indicates: If we are in PCV mode AND ventilation is on, and one of the additional conditions for transition is met, the system will switch to PSVMode.
- The second part of the requirement specifies that: The transition (from PCV to PSV) shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) if the change mode command (PSVModeSelected) has been received from the GUI.
- We interpret this as: Whenever (inspiratoryPhaseEnd AND PSVModeSelected AND PCVMode AND ventilating) hold, the system must (eventually) switch to PSVMode.

Since this is a *trigger*, a common LTL pattern is: Whenever an 'event' occurs (the conjunction), then eventually the 'effect' must hold. That is, G(event -> F(effect)).

For the sake of completeness, we assume PCVMode is an atomic proposition for the PCV mode being active, and PSVMode indicates the system has transitioned to PSVMode.

Putting this into LTL syntax:
G((PCVMode & ventilating & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PSVModeSelected) -> F(PSVMode))

Step-by-step translation: see below.

explanation_dict.natural_language: PCV mode is active and ventilation is on
End of a PCV inspiratory time is reached
The change mode command to PSV has been received from the GUI
Transition to PSV mode shall occur (i.e., system will be in PSVMode)
Globally, whenever PCV is active, ventilation is on, end-of-inspiration is reached, and PSV requested, then eventually the system reaches PSVMode

explanation:
First, break down the sentence: 'PCV Mode: The transition from PCV to PSV shall occur if ventilation is on, the transition from PCV to PSV shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time if the change mode command has been received from the GUI.'

Let:
- PCVMode: system is in PCV mode
- PSVModeSelected: change mode command to PSV received from GUI
- ventilating: system is ventilating (ventilation is on)
- inspiratoryPhaseEnd: end of inspiratory phase (end of PCV inspiratory time)

We interpret as follows:
- A transition from PCV to PSV can occur when (1) ventilation is on, (2) the command to change to PSV has been received, and (3) it happens at the end of the PCV inspiratory time.

Translating to LTL:
- Globally, if we are in PCV mode AND PSVModeSelected is true AND ventilating is true, then eventually at the end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), the mode shall change from PCV to PSV (i.e., not PCVMode and PSVMode).

Since we have no atomic proposition for an explicit 'transition', we use the condition that after the end of inspiratory phase, not PCVMode AND PSVMode holds if the other requirements are met.

This gives: G((PCVMode & PSVModeSelected & ventilating) -> F(inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X(!PCVMode & PSVMode)))

In other words, always, whenever these preconditions hold, then at the end of inspiratory phase, in the next state PCVMode is false and PSVMode is true.

explanation_dict.natural_language: ventilation is on
change mode command has been received from the GUI
the end of a PCV inspiratory time
in PCV mode
transition from PCV to PSV shall occur

output_LTL: G((PCVMode & PSVModeSelected & ventilating) -> F(inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X(!PCVMode & PSVMode)))

explanation:
The sentence expresses two requirements. First, the transition from PCV mode to PSV mode should be possible only when ventilation is on. Second, the transition shall occur at the END OF a PCV inspiratory time IF the command has been received from the GUI (i.e., PSVModeSelected and at the end of inspiratory phase). We'll represent being in PCV mode by PCVMode, being in PSV mode by PSVMode. 

First requirement: If currently in PCVMode, the system does NOT transition to PSVMode unless ventilation is on. In LTL: G( (PCVMode & X PSVMode) -> ventilating ).

Second requirement: This transition from PCV to PSV should only happen at the end of a PCV inspiratory time IF the mode-change command from the GUI is present. So: Whenever in PCVMode, a transition to PSVMode at the next step can only happen if (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PSVModeSelected) hold at this step. In LTL: G( (PCVMode & X PSVMode) -> (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PSVModeSelected) ).

Combining both, and since both are necessary, we use AND (&):
G( (PCVMode & X PSVMode) -> ventilating ) & G( (PCVMode & X PSVMode) -> (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PSVModeSelected) )

That is, globally, any time the system transitions from PCVMode to PSVMode, we require: (1) ventilation must be on, (2) the end of the inspiratory phase and the change-mode command must be present.

explanation_dict.natural_language: in PCV mode and next state PSV mode
ventilation is on
transition from PCV to PSV only if ventilating
end of PCV inspiratory time and change mode command received (from GUI): inspiratory phase ends and PSV mode selected
transition from PCV to PSV only at the end of inspiratory time and if change mode command received
Globally (always) apply these requirements

output_LTL: G((PCVMode & X PSVMode) -> ventilating) & G((PCVMode & X PSVMode) -> (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PSVModeSelected))

explanation:
We are asked to translate: 'PCV Mode: The transition from PCV to PSV shall occur if ventilation is on, the transition from PCV to PSV shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time if the change mode command has been received from the GUI.'

Step 1: Identify the relevant atomic propositions:
• 'PCV' means the system is in PCV Mode (PCVMode)
• 'PSV' means the system is in PSV Mode (PSVMode)
• 'ventilation is on' is 'ventilating'
• 'change mode command has been received from GUI' is 'PSVModeSelected'
• 'at the end of a PCV inspiratory time' is 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd'

Step 2: Identify what 'the transition from PCV to PSV' means:
This means that if we are currently in PCVMode and move to PSVMode in the next step (i.e., X(PSVMode) and PCVMode now), that is a transition event.

We can formulate a transition as: PCVMode & X(PSVMode)

Step 3: Consider the two sentences (decomposed):
1. 'The transition from PCV to PSV shall occur if ventilation is on.' => Whenever we are in PCVMode and ventilating is true, then only a transition to PSVMode (next state) can happen.
That is: G(((PCVMode & ventilating) & transition) -> TRUE).
But the original says: 'shall occur if', which means the transition is only allowed under these conditions.
Standard LTL fairness form is to express that whenever the condition occurs, a transition will eventually happen. However, English says "the transition shall occur if", which means sufficient conditions for the transition, so it's not "only if" (not necessary conditions), but rather that these conditions are sufficient.
So, equivalently, if the transition occurs, then the condition must have been true in the previous moment (this is typically how transitions are controlled), but LTL doesn't support past unless using future.
So the correct reading for our context is: Whenever (PCVMode & ventilating) holds, then (eventually) the system transitions to PSV (i.e., next state PSVMode).
So: G(((PCVMode & ventilating) & X(PSVMode)) -> TRUE) - this is tautologically true. Instead, the natural formalization is: G((PCVMode & ventilating) -> F (PCVMode & X(PSVMode))).
But that pattern would ensure that whenever the system is in PCV and ventilating, a transition to PSV will eventually occur (which seems not quite right, as perhaps the real meaning is: a transition can only occur if ventilating, or: whenever the transition occurs, ventilating was true). Given ambiguity, we consider the main aim - a transition from PCV to PSV can only occur if ventilating is true (invariant).
So: G((PCVMode & X(PSVMode)) -> ventilating)

2. 'the transition from PCV to PSV shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time if the change mode command has been received from the GUI.'
This reads: If the mode change command to PSV has been received, then at the end of a PCV inspiratory time, a transition to PSV mode must occur.
So: G( (PCVMode & PSVModeSelected) -> F( inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X(PSVMode) ) )
This says: whenever PCVMode is active and the user command is present, then eventually (at a time when) inspiratoryPhaseEnd holds, PSVMode must become true in the next step.
Alternatively, the requirement could be stricter: at the *first* inspiratoryPhaseEnd after the mode command, do the transition. Without more clarity, the above matches the description.

Putting both formulas together yields the combined specification (the two constraints must always hold, so we combine them with and):
G((PCVMode & X(PSVMode)) -> ventilating) & G((PCVMode & PSVModeSelected) -> F(inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X(PSVMode)))

explanation_dict.natural_language: transition from PCV to PSV
ventilation is on
transition from PCV to PSV shall occur if ventilation is on
transition from PCV to PSV shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time if the change mode command has been received from the GUI

output_LTL: G((PCVMode & X(PSVMode)) -> ventilating) & G((PCVMode & PSVModeSelected) -> F(inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X(PSVMode)))

explanation: The sentence describes a requirement for the transition from PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) mode to PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode. It states that the transition should occur if (1) ventilation is on and (2) a change mode command has been received from the GUI, and (3) it should happen at the end of the PCV inspiratory time. In terms of atomic propositions: 'ventilating' represents ventilation being on, 'PSVModeSelected' is the change mode command from the GUI, and 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' represents the end of the inspiratory phase in the PCV cycle. The specification says that globally, if ventilating is true and the change mode command for PSV is received, then at the end of the inspiratory phase, the mode should be switched (PSVMode becomes active). So, whenever all conditions are met, then at the next instant after inspiratoryPhaseEnd, PSVMode must be true. We encode this as: globally, whenever (ventilating & PSVModeSelected & inspiratoryPhaseEnd), then in the next instant, PSVMode holds.

explanation_dict.natural_language: Ventilation is on
Transition from PCV to PSV shall occur at the end of PCV inspiratory time if change mode command received from GUI
Globally (this must always hold)

explanation:
Let's parse the sentence in two main semantic parts: (1) 'The transition from PCV to PSV shall occur if ventilation is on', and (2) 'the transition from PCV to PSV shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time if the change mode command has been received from the GUI.'

We need an LTL formula expressing that from PCVMode, if ventilation is on (ventilating), then the transition to PSVMode can occur. Furthermore, more specifically, the transition from PCV to PSV must occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) if the mode change command (PSVModeSelected) has been received from the GUI, i.e., the PSVModeSelected is true.

This can be interpreted as: Whenever we are in PCVMode, and ventilation is on, then if inspiratoryPhaseEnd and PSVModeSelected both hold, then in the next state, we enter PSVMode.

We will model:
Let T := (in PCVMode) & ventilating & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PSVModeSelected -> X(PSVMode)

Now, to make sure this property is enforced globally (i.e., at all times), we use G.

Output formula: G((PCVMode & ventilating & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PSVModeSelected) -> X(PSVMode))

This covers:
- Only when all those conditions in PCV are met, does the transition to PSVMode happen in next state.
- "If ventilation is on..." is ensured by the conjunct 'ventilating' in the preconditions.

(If other (looser) interpretations are needed, they should be specified, but this matches most accurate system intent.)

explanation_dict.natural_language: in PCV mode
ventilation is on
transition occurs at end of PCV inspiratory time if change mode command received from GUI
globally

explanation: The specification describes when the transition from PCV mode to PSV mode should occur in a ventilator: (1) it should happen if ventilation is on (ventilating), and (2) specifically, at the end of a PCV inspiratory time (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if a mode change command from the GUI to PSV has been received (PSVModeSelected). To translate this to LTL step-by-step: (1) 'if ventilation is on' becomes precondition 'ventilating', (2) 'at the end of a PCV inspiratory time' is 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd', (3) 'change mode command received from GUI' is 'PSVModeSelected', (4) 'transition from PCV to PSV' is not directly an atomic proposition, so we'll define the condition for the transition as corresponding to these triggers. Thus, whenever ('ventilating' and 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' and 'PSVModeSelected'), then (eventually) 'PSVMode' should hold. To ensure this is required globally, wrap the implication with G. Thus, the LTL formula is: G((ventilating & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PSVModeSelected) -> F PSVMode).

explanation_dict.natural_language: ventilation is on
end of a PCV inspiratory time
change mode command has been received from the GUI
if ventilating, and at inspiratoryPhaseEnd, and change mode received, then (eventually) PSVMode occurs
globally (for all time)

explanation:
We interpret the requirement as follows: The system is in PCV Mode (PCVMode). A transition from PCV to PSV should occur if ventilation is on (ventilating) and the mode change command from the GUI to PSV has been received (PSVModeSelected). However, this transition (meaning PCVMode becomes false and PSVMode becomes true) is only allowed at the end of the PCV inspiratory time (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) if the mode change command has been received. In other words, whenever we are in PCVMode, if the conditions 'ventilating' and 'PSVModeSelected' are both true, then at the next occurrence of 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd', PSVMode must become true (i.e., the transition to PSV must be executed). 

We structure this as: Globally, whenever the system is ventilating and PSVModeSelected is true and we are in PCVMode, then eventually, at the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, PSVMode will be true.

In LTL, 'eventually at the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, PSVMode will be true' is captured as: (inspiratoryPhaseEnd -> X(PSVMode)), wrapped by F (i.e., there will be such an inspiratoryPhaseEnd in the future). The overall requirement is that globally (G), if PCVMode & ventilating & PSVModeSelected hold, it is guaranteed that eventually, at the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, PSVMode will hold after that instant. 

So, the formula is: G( (PCVMode & ventilating & PSVModeSelected) -> F(inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X(PSVMode)) ).

explanation_dict.natural_language: Ventilation is on
Change mode command to PSV has been received from GUI
System is in PCV Mode
If ventilating and PSVModeSelected and PCVMode, then ensure next inspiratoryPhaseEnd causes transition to PSVMode
Globally

explanation:
First, let's break down the original sentence: 'The transition from PCV to PSV shall occur if ventilation is on, the transition from PCV to PSV shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time if the change mode command has been received from the GUI.'

- The first part says: If ventilation is on ('ventilating'), then a transition from PCV to PSV (i.e., PSVMode becomes active) is permitted.
- The second part refines when this transition takes place: It shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time ('inspiratoryPhaseEnd') if the change mode command ('PSVModeSelected') has been received from the GUI.

So, when we are ventilating, a mode change to PSV should only occur at the end of the PCV inspiratory phase and only if the GUI triggers the change. That is: whenever ventilation is running and the user asks for PSVMode, at the end of the current inspiratory phase, PSVMode should be entered.

Let us express this in LTL step by step:
1. 'If ventilating and change to PSVMode is selected, then at the next end of inspiratory phase, PSVMode should be active.'
2. Formally: (ventilating & PSVModeSelected) -> F (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X PSVMode).
3. 'Globally', i.e., always during the system execution, this should be enforced: G((ventilating & PSVModeSelected) -> F (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X PSVMode)).

This formula asserts that whenever ventilating and the user selects PSVMode, then it will eventually (F), at the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, trigger a transition to PSVMode (i.e., PSVMode will be true in the next time instant after inspiratoryPhaseEnd).

Caveat: If you interpret 'at the end of a PCV inspiratory time' strictly, you may want to assert that the transition always occurs only at these points. More strictly, each transition to PSVMode implies that there was previously an inspiratoryPhaseEnd with the above conditions. But following the way the English is worded, the sufficiency of the condition is adequately captured here.

In summary, the formula is:
G((ventilating & PSVModeSelected) -> F (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X PSVMode)).

explanation_dict.natural_language: ventilation is on
change mode command has been received from the GUI
at the end of a PCV inspiratory time
transition from PCV to PSV shall occur
if ventilation is on and PSV change selected, then eventually, at inspiratoryPhaseEnd, PSVMode is next
Globally (always enforce)
